utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format includes 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. In the final coverage plan, all 5 of these items are present. Each item is fully specified with detailed descriptions and associated costs. health_coverage includes both physical and mental health breakdowns with costs; property_coverage specifies home/contents insurance with costs; travel_coverage includes international travel insurance with costs; additional_services lists multiple enhancements and their costs; total_budget provides both monthly and annual cost breakdowns. Therefore, all required items are included and fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists: (1) physical_health coverage costing 120 EUR/month with comprehensive inclusions; (2) mental_health coverage costing 65 EUR/month; (3) home_contents coverage at 90 EUR/month; and (4) international_travel coverage at 50 EUR/month. Comparing these with the ground-truth ratings: For physical_health at 120 EUR/month, the closest plan is HealthFirst Standard Plan (110 EUR) or LifeFirst Standard Plan (100 EUR), but none match 120 EUR exactly. However, the included features (Executive Health Programs, Preventive Care, Dental, Vision, Maternity) indicate a higher-end plan, so HealthFirst Standard Plan (rating 7) fits best. For mental_health at 65 EUR/month, LifeFirst Standard Plan costs exactly 65 EUR with rating 8. For property coverage home_contents at 90 EUR/month, PrimeHome Premium Coverage costs 90 EUR with rating 10. For travel coverage international_travel at 50 EUR/month, InsurePlus Standard Plan has the same monthly cost of 50 EUR with rating 7. The additional services are bundled and free; they align with general premium add-ons like concierge, priority claims, and telemedicine, which are covered under high-tier plans but have no cost rating separately.
